---
title: "Recipe 3: Axis of Diversification"
output: html_document
date: "2026-05-30"
---
```{r}
# ── Recipe 3: Axis of Diversification ──────────────────────────
source("https://michaelalfaro.github.io/eeb187-demos/recipes/code/helpers.R")   # loads ape, phytools, ggplot2, theme_poster

# ── 1. Load your bundle and pavo data ──────────────────────────
bundle   <- load_bundle("/Users/Flo/Downloads/EEB187/scarinae-78-bundle")

# Strip specimen codes from tree tips
bundle$tree$tip.label <- gsub("^([A-Za-z]+_[a-z]+).*", "\\1", bundle$tree$tip.label)

# Remove any duplicate tips created by stripping (keep first occurrence)
dups <- duplicated(bundle$tree$tip.label)
if (any(dups)) {
  cat("Dropping", sum(dups), "duplicate tips after stripping\n")
  bundle$tree <- ape::drop.tip(bundle$tree, bundle$tree$tip.label[dups])
}
pavo_csv <- read.csv("/Users/Flo/Downloads/EEB187/traits_df_78.csv",
                      stringsAsFactors = FALSE)




# ── 2. Match tree to data ──────────────────────────────────────
matched <- match_tree_data(bundle$tree, pavo_csv, species_col = "species")
tree    <- matched$tree
dat     <- matched$data

# ── 3. Build trait matrix ──────────────────────────────────────
pavo_vars <- c("m", "A", "Jc", "Jt", "m_dS", "m_dL")
trait_mat <- dat[, pavo_vars]
rownames(trait_mat) <- dat$species

# Drop rows with any NA
complete  <- complete.cases(trait_mat)
trait_mat <- trait_mat[complete, ]
tree_pca  <- ape::drop.tip(tree,
                            setdiff(tree$tip.label, rownames(trait_mat)))
trait_mat <- trait_mat[tree_pca$tip.label, ]   # reorder to match tree

cat("phyloPCA on", nrow(trait_mat), "species x",
    ncol(trait_mat), "variables\n")

# ── 4. Run phyloPCA ───────────────────────────────────────────
phy_pc <- phytools::phyl.pca(tree_pca, trait_mat,
                              method = "lambda", mode = "corr")

# ── 5. Print loadings for PC1–PC3 ─────────────────────────────
cat("\n── Loadings (which variable drives each axis) ──\n")
loadings_mat <- phy_pc$L
print(round(loadings_mat[, 1:min(3, ncol(loadings_mat))], 3))

# Identify the dominant variable on each axis
for (i in 1:min(3, ncol(loadings_mat))) {
  top_var <- names(which.max(abs(loadings_mat[, i])))
  cat(sprintf("  PC%d dominated by: %s (loading = %.3f)\n",
              i, top_var, loadings_mat[top_var, i]))
}

# ── 6. Print variance explained ───────────────────────────────
eigenvalues <- diag(phy_pc$Eval)
var_pct     <- round(100 * eigenvalues / sum(eigenvalues), 1)
cat("\n── Variance explained ──\n")
for (i in seq_along(var_pct)) {
  cat(sprintf("  PC%d: %.1f%%\n", i, var_pct[i]))
}
cat(sprintf("  PC1 + PC2: %.1f%%\n", sum(var_pct[1:2])))

# ── 7. Build the hero biplot ──────────────────────────────────
# Extract scores and add genus labels
scores <- as.data.frame(phy_pc$S[, 1:2])
colnames(scores) <- c("PC1", "PC2")
scores$species <- rownames(scores)
scores$genus   <- sub("_.*", "", scores$species)

# Loading arrows — scale to fit the plot
L <- as.data.frame(loadings_mat[, 1:2])
colnames(L) <- c("PC1", "PC2")
L$variable <- rownames(L)
arrow_scale <- 0.85 *
  min(diff(range(scores$PC1)), diff(range(scores$PC2))) / 2 /
  max(sqrt(L$PC1^2 + L$PC2^2))

p <- ggplot(scores, aes(x = PC1, y = PC2, color = genus)) +
  # Species points
  geom_point(size = 4, alpha = 0.85) +
  # Species labels (abbreviated)
  geom_text(aes(label = sub("^(\\w{4})\\w*_(\\w{4}).*", "\\1_\\2",
                             species)),
            size = 2.8, vjust = -1.2, show.legend = FALSE) +
  # Loading arrows
  geom_segment(data = L,
               aes(x = 0, y = 0,
                   xend = PC1 * arrow_scale,
                   yend = PC2 * arrow_scale),
               arrow = arrow(length = unit(0.25, "cm")),
               color = "#c14a4a", linewidth = 1, inherit.aes = FALSE) +
  # Loading labels
  geom_text(data = L,
            aes(x = PC1 * arrow_scale * 1.15,
                y = PC2 * arrow_scale * 1.15,
                label = variable),
            color = "#c14a4a", fontface = "bold", size = 4.5,
            inherit.aes = FALSE) +
  # Axis labels with variance explained
  labs(
    x = sprintf("phyloPC1  (%.1f%% variance)", var_pct[1]),
    y = sprintf("phyloPC2  (%.1f%% variance)", var_pct[2]),
    title = "Axis of color pattern diversification",
    subtitle = "phyloPCA biplot — arrows show pavo variable loadings",
    color = "Genus"
  ) +
  coord_equal() +
  theme_poster

print(p)

# ── 8. Save poster-quality PDF ─────────────────────────────────
ggsave("results/recipe3-phylopca-biplot.pdf", p, width = 12, height = 10)
cat("Saved: results/recipe3-phylopca-biplot.pdf\n")
```
